summary: Tested the new AI Automation Generator endpoint + UI, and self_heal field on the Real User Traffic jobs endpoint. 5/6 backend pytest cases passed; the only failure is the actual Gemini 2.5 Pro call which rejects with 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0525175, Max budget: 0.001' on the configured EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. All validation paths (auth, invalid file type, >1 video), self_heal=true/false field acceptance, and the full UI flow (open toggle, fields visible, file upload, request hits backend) pass. Frontend gracefully shows the LLM error as a toast, so code is correct — the blocker is purely the LLM key/budget.

critical.issue: Real Gemini 2.5 Pro call fails with 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0525175, Max budget: 0.001' via litellm/OpenAIException. This is an Emergent LLM Key balance/budget configuration issue, not a code defect. Endpoint wiring, sanitiser, multipart parsing and error surfacing all work. Please top up / reconfigure the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget so the feature can actually generate steps.

integration_issues.issue: End-to-end JSON population into automationJson textarea could not be verified because the LLM call itself is rejected by the budget limit (same root cause as backend). Frontend correctly surfaces the error via toast; when Gemini actually responds with steps, code at RealUserTrafficPage.js:231-235 will set useCustomJson=true and pretty-print the JSON.

action_items: Top up / raise the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget (currently Max budget: 0.001 which is immediately exceeded on the first Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal call). Once fixed, rerun test_happy_path_with_png_returns_steps and manually click Generate in the UI to confirm automationJson textarea is populated.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:3376 imports `tempfile` inline inside the router module — fine but should be moved to the top of file imports.
server.py:3410-3429 silently skips unsupported files (kind is None). Good for mixed uploads, but consider logging the skip so debugging is easier.
ai_automation_generator.py correctly uses async, sanitises actions against a whitelist, and strips ```json fences. Well structured.
Frontend: `onAiGenerate` does client-side validation for >15 images and >1 video which mirrors backend — good UX.
Minor: the image-count validation in onAiGenerate (imageCount>15) uses all selected files including videos in a second Array.from; logic is correct but could be simplified.

success_rate.backend: 83% (5/6 — 1 failure is external LLM budget, not code)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of verifiable UI paths (integration into success-state textarea blocked by same LLM budget)

seed_data_creation: Generated an in-memory PNG (mock form with UNLOCK NOW / First/Last/Email / SUBMIT) at /tmp/mock_form.png via PIL for both pytest and Playwright tests. Auto-cleaned per test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Once EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is raised, rerun: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_ai_automation_generator.py::TestAiAutomationGenerator::test_happy_path_with_png_returns_steps -v` and verify it returns status=ok with >=1 step whose action is in the allowed whitelist. Also do a manual UI verification that the automationJson textarea gets populated and the useCustomJson checkbox becomes checked after Generate. All other test paths (validation 400s, self_heal true/false on jobs endpoint, UI rendering of AI generator section) are already green.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the only failure: the configured EMERGENT_LLM_KEY has a max budget of 0.001 USD which is immediately blown by a single Gemini 2.5 Pro multimodal (image) call (cost ≈ 0.052 USD). litellm returns BadRequestError which the backend cleanly wraps into {status:'failed', error:'AI call failed: ...Budget has been exceeded...'}. The Gemini-2.5-pro model name, emergentintegrations.LlmChat wiring, multipart upload handling, sanitiser and UI toast error flow are all verified correct. Mitigation: raise the budget cap on the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (user action).
